documents.text: API liveness health endpoint is GET /health.
RustFS serves source objects locally as the S3-compatible object store.
PostgreSQL is the authoritative store for metadata.
Redis is the Celery broker and result backend.
Qdrant and Neo4j are rebuildable projections and are not authoritative.
Caddy routes browser API calls to the API service.
Start the local stack with docker compose up -d.
Datasets are isolated by mandatory project_id and dataset_id filters in PostgreSQL and Qdrant.
Indexing moves through queued, reading, parsing, chunking, embedding, persisting, and complete; failures are recorded and retries are idempotent.
Stable identifiers are formed from SHA-256 hashes of document ID, source hash, pipeline version, and chunk index.
Document upload accepts UTF-8 TXT, Markdown, HTML, CSV, and valid PDF files.
Duplicate uploads in a dataset are deduplicated by content hash and return the existing document.
Celery indexing jobs execute in the worker service.
Source documents are stored once in S3-compatible object storage; PostgreSQL stores authoritative object keys and hashes.
Providers must support embeddings and chat completion with usage reporting; generated citations are validated.
